steps.title: Focus on a narrative opening
steps.content: The student wants to begin a narrative about Oberon's life. A strong opening should establish the starting point of that life: the person, birthplace, and birth year, rather than jumping directly to later career events.

steps.title: Avoid career-only details
steps.content: Choice B summarizes several career facts, Choice C jumps to one role in 1941, and Choice D focuses on early uncredited film work. Those are useful later, but they do not begin the life story. The option that starts in Mumbai in 1911 best opens the narrative. The answer is A.
